reference: My five-year-old son woke up this morning with a hard lump on the left side of his forehead. Now, this afternoon, he has developed an even larger lump on the right side of his head. He is allergic to grass, and he does have eczema. He occasionally gets a little puffy with an insect bite; however, there are no traces of an insect bite—just two hard lumps on either side of his head that are a little tender to the touch. What would be the best thing to do?
text: My five-year-old son woke up this morning with a hard lump on the left side of his forehead. Now, this afternoon, he has developed an even larger lump on the right side of his head. He is allergic to grass, and he does have eczema. He occasionally gets a little puffy with an insect bite. However, there are no traces of an insect bite. Just two hard lumps on either side of his head that are a little tender to the touch. What would be the best thing to do?
